project.name: TUGAS-AKHIR-VAR-TATA-SURYA
project.currentFocus: Validate contained selected-planet GLB detail preview, deterministic return to solar overview, and mobile close-to-landing hard reset across devices.

lastAchievements: Removed the AR-scene detail zoom path so tapping a planet no longer renders a large selected planet behind the info panel.
Made Kembali ke Tata Surya deterministic: it hides the panel, clears the detail root, resets scales, and shows the controlled solar fallback row again.
Reduced the embedded GLB preview slot and forced its A-Frame canvas to stay inside the panel under the return button.
Added mobile/touch close fallback: after stream/artifact cleanup and landing remount, the page reloads to clear persistent AR.js browser side effects.
Expanded cleanupAFrameArtifacts to remove all A-Frame scenes during teardown, including preview scenes.
Updated DESIGN, design-intent, flow overview, project brief, and ADR to match contained preview, deterministic return, and mobile close reload behavior.
pendingIssues: Real-device verification is still required on Android Chrome, iOS Safari, and tablets after the deterministic return and mobile close reload patch.
If the solar row is still too small or too large, adjust getSolarScaleMultiplier or SOLAR_FALLBACK_SCALE from the current 0.42 baseline.
If planet taps feel offset after scale changes, tune hitZonePosition and hitZoneRadius in src/data/planets.ts.
Verify embedded GLB preview sizing across planets; tweak previewScale or PANEL_PREVIEW_TARGET_SIZE values if any appear too large or too small.
If close still fails on a specific browser after the mobile reload fallback, capture whether reload fired and whether the deployed service worker/cache served stale assets.
nextBestActions: Deploy and test open AR -> scan Hiro -> tap planet -> confirm contained local GLB preview under the return button -> Kembali ke Tata Surya -> confirm solar row returns.
Tap close on Android/iOS and confirm the page reloads back to a normal landing page.
Verify the landing page and modal remain centered and tappable after closing AR and after reopening AR.
